# Raw To Draft Rules

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/provideradapteracldocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to adapter ACL](../adapter-acl.md)

Adapter parsers own provider-local `RawEvent` envelopes. Adapter translation maps those envelopes into provider-neutral run-scope events; supervisor draft construction maps those run events into canonical IR event types.

1. Known provider raw types map to registered domain `EventType` values through adapter translation and `providerEventDraft`.
2. Unknown provider raw types must become non-transition provider observations or logs; they must not create FSM authority.
3. Known raw types with unknown fields preserve unknown source data only through explicitly modeled draft payload/unknown surfaces.
4. Derived meaning must be marked by the translating adapter when provider output did not directly report the fact.
5. Provider transition-after-side facts are drafts only; ingest/orchestration decides state transitions.

The adjacent [`event-ingestor-contract.md`](event-ingestor-contract.md) owns final `CanonicalEvent` envelope completion and validation.
